steps.title: Identify what timeframe the blank describes
steps.content: The opening phrase refers to a past event (recognition in 2000), but the blank must describe what the area IS now — its current, defining nature.

steps.title: Apply the verb tense rule
steps.content: The rule is that present tense expresses ongoing states and defining characteristics, distinct from past events. Present tense (is) correctly describes the area's current nature as a region with these attributes. The other choices either misalign tense to the past (was), suggest the state has ended (had been), or are temporally nonsensical (will have been). That matches choice C — C is correct.
